heading: Continuation of pay; election to use annual or sick leave
label: 8118 Continuation of pay; election to use annual or sick leave
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 758, .Sept. 7, 1916, ch. 458, § 839 Stat. 743, .Oct. 14, 1949, ch. 691, § 101(b)63 Stat. 854Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

Amendments2006—Subsec. (b)(1). amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (1) read as follows: “without a break in time unless controverted under regulations of the Secretary;”.Pub. L. 109–4351974— inserted in section catchline the reference to continuation of pay, added subsecs. (a), (b), (d) and (e), designated existing provisions as subsec. (c), and in subsec. (c) as so designated, substituted “until termination of pay as set forth in subsections (a) and (b) or the use of annual or sick leave ends” for “until the use of the annual or sick leave ends”.Pub. L. 93–416

children.label: a
children.text: The United States shall authorize the continuation of pay of an employee, as defined in (other than those referred to in clause (B) or (E)), who has filed a claim for a period of wage loss due to a traumatic injury with his immediate superior on a form approved by the Secretary of Labor within the time specified in .section 8101(1) of this titlesection 8122(a)(2) of this title
without a break in time, except as provided under section 8117(b), unless controverted under regulations of the Secretary;
for a period not to exceed 45 days; and
under accounting procedures and such other regulations as the Secretary may require.

children.label: c
children.text: An employee may use annual or sick leave to his credit at the time the disability begins, but his compensation for disability does not begin, and the time periods specified by do not begin to run, until termination of pay as set forth in subsections (a) and (b) or the use of annual or sick leave ends.section 8117 of this title

children.label: d
children.text: If a claim under subsection (a) is denied by the Secretary, payments under this section shall, at the option of the employee, be charged to sick or annual leave or shall be deemed overpayments of pay within the meaning of , United States Code.section 5584 of title 5

children.label: e
children.text: Payments under this section shall not be considered as compensation as defined by .section 8101(12) of this title
